inline_comments: MSTeams-specific media size limit (100MB). Higher than the default because OneDrive upload handles large files well.

Send a message to a Teams conversation or user. Uses the stored ConversationReference from previous interactions. The bot must have received at least one message from the conversation before proactive messaging works. File handling by conversation type: - Personal (1:1) chats: small images (<4MB) use base64, large files and non-images use FileConsentCard - Group chats / channels: files are uploaded to OneDrive and shared via link
Handle media if present
Personal chats: base64 only works for images; use FileConsentCard for large files or non-images
Personal chat with small image: use base64 (only works for images)
Small image in personal chat: use base64 (only works for images)
Group chat/channel without SharePoint: send image inline (avoids OneDrive failures)
Group chat or channel: upload to SharePoint (if siteId configured) or OneDrive
Use SharePoint upload + Graph API for native file card
Get driveItem properties needed for native file card
Build native Teams file card attachment and send via Bot Framework
Fallback: no SharePoint site configured, use OneDrive with markdown link
Send message with file link (Bot Framework doesn't support "reference" attachment type for sending)
No media: send text only
Send a text message with optional base64 media URL.
Send a poll (Adaptive Card) to a Teams conversation or user.
Send poll via proactive conversation (Adaptive Cards require direct activity send)
Send an arbitrary Adaptive Card to a Teams conversation or user.
Send card via proactive conversation
List all known conversation references (for debugging/CLI).

functions.name: sendMessageMSTeams
functions.doc: Send a message to a Teams conversation or user.     Uses the stored ConversationReference from previous interactions.

functions.name: sendTextWithMedia
functions.doc: Send a text message with optional base64 media URL.

functions.name: sendPollMSTeams
functions.doc: Send a poll (Adaptive Card) to a Teams conversation or user.

functions.name: sendAdaptiveCardMSTeams
functions.doc: Send an arbitrary Adaptive Card to a Teams conversation or user.

functions.name: listMSTeamsConversations
functions.doc: List all known conversation references (for debugging).

concerns: Multiple concerns detected: ui, api, business_logic, auth, logging
